description: grid-template-areas named placement: a grid-area name pins an item to the rectangle its name spans in the template, independent of DOM order. Covers a holy-grail layout (spanning header/footer, a fr row taking the container's leftover height) and a template with an empty (.) cell plus two-row column-spanning areas.
visual_assertions: Header spans the full width on the top row; Sidebar (left, 80pt) and Maincontent (right, 1fr) share the middle row; Footer spans the full width on the bottom row — even though Maincontent precedes Sidebar in the DOM.
The middle 1fr row takes the container's leftover height (200pt minus the two 30pt rows and the two 8pt gaps).
In the board grid, Acol is placed in the left column (both rows), Bcol in the right column (both rows), and Cmid only in the lower-middle cell — the upper-middle cell is the empty '.'.
